error_signature: execution stops if rows do not exist
title: Execution stops when trying to upsert non-existent rows
symptom: When attempting to upsert rows in the Counter table, the execution stops if a key does not exist in the Counter table, leading to incomplete counts.
applies_when: Counter table may have missing keys
not_this_if: All keys exist in the Counter table
root_cause: The Upsert Rows node relies on the output of a GET node to determine if a row exists. If the key does not exist, the GET node returns no output, causing the Upsert Rows node to not execute for that key, resulting in incomplete data.
fix_steps: Add a Merge node before the Upsert Rows node
Connect both branches of your workflow to the Merge node
Ensure the Upsert Rows node is connected to the output of the Merge node
agent_instruction: Insert a Merge node before the Upsert Rows node to prevent execution stops.
